macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Wall Street Falls as Middle East Tensions and Tech Earnings Loom
items.summary: U.S. major indexes closed lower on Monday: Dow fell 0.59% to 51,839.26, S&P 500 dropped 0.19% to 7,443.28, and Nasdaq declined 0.05% to 25,508.07 amid rising oil prices and geopolitical fears [8][11].

items.title: Oil Surges Past $90 Before Easing on Iran Diplomacy Hopes
items.summary: Brent crude jumped ~3% to top $90/barrel on Monday due to U.S.-Iran hostilities disrupting Strait of Hormuz outlook, then eased slightly as Iran signaled diplomacy remains possible [2][4].

items.title: Trump Imposes 50% Tariffs on Canadian Goods Over Trade Discrimination
items.summary: The U.S. announced 50% tariffs on wide Canadian products including dairy, alcohol, and cars, effective in 30 days, citing Canada's discriminatory treatment of U.S. exports [1][5].

items.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Vows to Defeat Inflation and Shrink Balance Sheet
items.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh pledged Tuesday to return inflation to the 2% target and faces the challenge of reducing the Fed's $6.7T balance sheet without destabilizing banks [5][6].

items.title: Inflation Slowed to 3.5% in June as Gas Prices Fell
items.summary: U.S. inflation dropped to 3.5% in June, below the 3.8% analyst forecast, providing consumers a break from rising gasoline prices [3].

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot with a $4.88T valuation after Nvidia's market value dropped 3.5%, marking a shift in the AI trade leadership [4].

items.title: Morgan Stanley Posts Record Quarterly Revenue Amid Equities Trading Surge
items.summary: Morgan Stanley reported record quarterly revenue and profit as equities trading volume surged 69%, signaling strong Wall Street activity [5].

items.title: China's Q2 GDP Growth Slows to 4.3%, Weakest Since 2022
items.summary: China's economy grew 4.3% in Q2, the slowest pace since 2022, despite strong exports and a $1.2T trade surplus, raising concerns over domestic job and spending lag [6][10].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP Gain Amid US-Iran Hostilities; BTC Holds $65K
items.summary: Leading cryptocurrencies traded mixed with Bitcoin up 0.86% at $65,438, Ethereum up 1.51% at $1,909, and XRP up 1.30% at $1.11 as escalating geopolitical tensions curbed risk appetite, though BTC reclaimed $65K with 92% higher volume.

items.title: SEC Sues Mining Automatic and Founder Over Alleged $22M Crypto Mining Scheme
items.summary: The SEC filed charges against Mining Automatic and its CEO for allegedly operating a $22 million fraudulent crypto mining scheme, marking a significant regulatory enforcement action.

items.title: Grayscale Files for Spot Worldcoin ETF as WLD Price Rises
items.summary: Wall Street asset manager Grayscale officially registered an S-1 filing with the SEC for a spot ETF tracking Worldcoin’s WLD token, prompting a price increase for the asset.

items.title: Circle Mints 250M USDC on Solana; Total 2026 Minting Reaches $71B
items.summary: Circle minted another 250 million USDC on the Solana blockchain, bringing its cumulative USDC minting on Solana in 2026 to $71.01 billion, signaling strong demand for stablecoins on the network.

items.title: Trump Agrees to Include Ethical Provisions in Broader Cryptocurrency Bill
items.summary: President Trump agreed to incorporate ethical provisions into the broader cryptocurrency legislation, though his stance has left CLARITY Act negotiations in limbo ahead of a potential Senate vote.

items.title: Russia Votes on Sweeping Crypto Regulations with $3,800 Annual Investor Cap
items.summary: Russia is set to vote on new crypto regulations that would impose a yearly investor cap of $3,800, representing a major shift in the country's digital asset policy.

items.title: United Stables Selects Chainlink for $1B Stablecoin Expansion
items.summary: United Stables has selected Chainlink as its official data oracle to support a planned $1 billion expansion of its stablecoin, enhancing data reliability for the project.

items.title: Stellar’s Tokenized RWA Market Cap Soars to $3.1 Billion
items.summary: Stellar’s market cap for tokenized real-world assets (RWA) has surged to $3.1 billion, highlighting growing adoption of the blockchain for asset tokenization.

items.title: DADDY Coin Plunges 26% Following US Arrest of Andrew and Tristan Tate
items.summary: The meme coin DADDY Coin dropped 26% after Andrew and Tristan Tate were arrested in the US, causing a sharp sell-off in the token tied to their brand.

items.title: Polymarket Refunds Users After Hackers Steal Crypto
items.summary: Polymarket announced it is refunding some users after hackers successfully stole cryptocurrency from the platform, marking a notable security incident in the prediction market sector.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Secures $1.5B Settlement in Authors' AI Copyright Case
items.summary: A federal judge in San Francisco approved Anthropic's landmark $1.5 billion settlement with authors who accused the company of misusing their books to train its Claude chatbot, ending a major class-action lawsuit [1][7].

items.title: Moonshot AI Pauses Kimi K3 Subscriptions Amid Capacity Strain
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI temporarily halted new subscriptions for its newly launched Kimi K3 model—a 2.8 trillion-parameter open-weight system claiming to be the world's largest open AI—as demand overwhelmed its infrastructure [1][5][9].

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Frontier Models
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5 and reinstated access to its Fable and Mythos frontier models after they were temporarily restricted following a federal export control review [6].

items.title: Google Developing 'Frozen v2' Chip with Embedded Gemini Architecture
items.summary: Google is building a new server chip called 'Frozen v2' that permanently etches parts of its Gemini model into silicon to improve efficiency, projected to launch by 2028 with 6–10x better performance per watt than current TPUs [1][8].

items.title: NVIDIA Ships Cosmos 3 Edge World Model for On-Device Robotics
items.summary: NVIDIA introduced Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B-parameter world model running on-device for vision reasoning and robot policy generation across Jetson, RTX, and DGX systems, with 22 Japanese robotics firms signing on [4].

items.title: Sakana AI Releases Fugu-Cyber for Enterprise Cybersecurity
items.summary: Sakana AI launched Fugu-Cyber, a multi-agent orchestration model for cybersecurity that achieves 86.9% on the CyberGym benchmark and matches frontier models like GPT-5 [8].

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-Red for Automated Red-Teaming
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Red, an automated red-teaming model trained via self-play reinforcement learning that reduced prompt injection failures by 6x in GPT-5.6 Sol compared to models from four months prior [8].

items.title: Tempus AI Acquires Personalis in $1.5B Precision Medicine Deal
items.summary: Tempus AI announced an all-stock acquisition of Personalis valued at approximately $1.5 billion, adding a cancer test to its AI-enabled oncology portfolio [1].

items.title: Trump Administration Weighs Restrictions on Chinese AI Models
items.summary: The Trump administration is considering measures to restrict Chinese AI models like Kimi K3, including adding labs to the Commerce Department's Entity List, to benefit U.S. firms like OpenAI and Anthropic [8].

items.title: Alibaba's Qwen-Audio-3.0-TTS Advances Real-Time Speech Expression
items.summary: Alibaba's Tongyi Qianwen launched Qwen-Audio-3.0-TTS, a real-time text-to-speech model that advances from basic speech to expressive vocal output [7].

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Activision Announces Call of Duty: NEXT Showcase for Modern Warfare 4
items.summary: Activision officially announced the upcoming Call of Duty: NEXT showcase scheduled for August 21, 2026, which will debut live gameplay and details for Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4[3].

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced Surpasses 3 Million Copies Sold
items.summary: The resynced version of Assassin's Creed: Black Flag has surpassed three million copies sold globally, marking a significant milestone for the franchise[4][7][9].

items.title: FaZe Vegas Wins Call of Duty League 2026 Championship
items.summary: FaZe Vegas defeated OpTic Texas in the grand finale of the Call of Duty League 2026 championship, securing the title[9].

items.title: Midgar Studio Ceases Operations
items.summary: Midgar Studio, known for Edge of Eternity and Edge of Memories, has officially ceased operations as announced by founder Jérémy Zeler-Maury[3].

items.title: NVIDIA Releases RTX Spark Toolkit with Windows on Arm Support
items.summary: NVIDIA released the RTX Spark Toolkit on July 20, 2026, alongside support for native Windows on Arm development[3].

items.title: Gallipoli Military Shooter Set for August 20 Release
items.summary: BlackMill Games confirmed the release date for the military shooter Gallipoli as August 20, 2026[6].

items.title: Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4 Gets New Necron Trailer
items.summary: Deep Silver and KING Art Games released a new cinematic trailer for Warhammer 40,000: Dawn of War 4, highlighting the Necrons' return, with a PC release set for September 17, 2026[3].

items.title: Tropico 7 New Trailer Debuted at Games Composed 2026
items.summary: Kalypso Media debuted a new trailer for the city-building strategy game Tropico 7 as part of the Games Composed in Germany 2026 initiative[3].

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Amiibo Figures Release Date Confirmed
items.summary: Resident Evil Requiem amiibo figures will be available at the Nintendo Store starting July 30, 2026[7][9].

items.title: Ghost Recon: Wildlands Upgraded to 4K and 60FPS on Consoles
items.summary: Ghost Recon: Wildlands appears to be receiving a 4K and 60FPS speed upgrade for console versions[7][9].

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Dethrones Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple has surpassed Nvidia to become the world's most valuable company, ending the chipmaker's long run at the top of the market.

items.title: Samsung Launches First U.S. Co-Branded Credit Card with Barclays
items.summary: Samsung Electronics launched its first U.S. credit card co-branded with Barclays, marking a significant expansion into financial services in the country.

items.title: TSMC Pledges Additional $100 Billion to Expand U.S. Chipmaking Capacity
items.summary: Taiwan chipmaker TSMC announced plans to invest another $100 billion to expand its manufacturing operations in the United States.

items.title: South Korea Plans New Semiconductor Hub in Southwest Within Four Years
items.summary: South Korea aims to establish a new semiconductor hub in its southwest region within four years, though local opposition to power and water demands poses a major obstacle.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Moratorium on Data Centers
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, marking the country's first statewide moratorium.

items.title: Netflix Paid $587 Million for Ben Affleck's AI Filmmaking Startup
items.summary: Netflix revealed it paid $587 million in cash for InterPositive, a startup co-founded by Ben Affleck focused on AI filmmaking.

items.title: Honda Prologue Will No Longer Be Sold in the U.S.
items.summary: The Honda Prologue electric vehicle will be discontinued in the U.S., joining a growing list of EV models exiting the market this year.

items.title: States Pause Paramount's $110 Billion Acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery
items.summary: A coalition of states led by California won a pause on Paramount's $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery after arguing it would harm competition.

items.title: Hackers Exploit Recently Patched WordPress Bugs, Putting Millions at Risk
items.summary: Two critical security flaws in WordPress software are being exploited by hackers, potentially allowing remote takeover of tens of millions of websites.

items.title: China and Thailand Pledge Expanded Technology and Innovation Cooperation
items.summary: China and Thailand committed to deeper cooperation in technology and innovation, including policy alignment and expanded exchanges in related sectors.
